key_theorem.theorem: QFT Confinement = EML Phase Transition Theorem
key_theorem.statement: The confinement transition in QCD is an EML-∞ transition: the running coupling g(μ) is EML-2 for μ > Λ_QCD (perturbative regime) and EML-∞ at μ = Λ_QCD (coupling diverges). This is exactly the EML Phase Transition Theorem (Session 57) applied to QFT: the free energy analog (effective potential V_eff) becomes EML-∞ at the confinement scale.
key_theorem.corollary_instantons: Non-perturbative QFT effects (instantons) are EML-∞ in the coupling g: exp(-8π²/g²) has an essential singularity at g=0 — it is invisible to all orders of perturbation theory (Taylor expansion in g). The EML-∞ barrier separates perturbative (EML-2) from non-perturbative physics.

one_loop_integral.reason: ln(Λ/m) = EML-2 (ln gate); Λ² = EML-0 (polynomial in cutoff)

mass_correction.reason: λ·ln(Λ/m) — coupling times EML-2 log = EML-2

qcd_asymptotic_freedom.eml_uv: EML-2: g²(μ)→0 as μ→∞ (asymptotic freedom = EML-2 running)
qcd_asymptotic_freedom.eml_ir: EML-∞: g²(μ)→∞ as μ→Λ_QCD (confinement = EML Phase Transition)
qcd_asymptotic_freedom.theorem: Confinement EML Theorem: The confinement transition in QCD occurs at μ=Λ_QCD where g(μ)→∞. This is an EML-∞ transition in exactly the sense of the EML Phase Transition Theorem (Session 57): the coupling g(μ) as a function of μ becomes EML-∞ at μ=Λ_QCD.

instanton_amplitude.formula: A_instanton = exp(-8π²/g²) [EML-∞ in g as g→0]
instanton_amplitude.reason: exp(-8π²/g²) has an essential singularity at g=0 — not expandable in powers of g

phi4_action.reason: Polynomial in φ up to degree 4 → EML-2

loop_integral_log.reason: Logarithmic UV divergence: ln gate → EML-2

landau_pole.description: λ(μ*)→∞ at Landau pole μ* = μ₀exp(1/(β₀λ₀))
landau_pole.reason: EML Phase Transition: coupling diverges → EML-∞

qcd_confinement.reason: EML Phase Transition: g diverges at Λ_QCD — same EML-∞ class as phase transitions (Session 57)

instanton_amplitude.reason: Essential singularity at g=0: cannot be expressed as EML-finite function of g

renormalized_mass.description: m_R² = m² + δm² where δm² ~ λln(Λ/m)
renormalized_mass.reason: Linear in λ times EML-2 (log) = EML-2

eml_depth_summary.EML-0: Coupling constants λ, g at vertices; color factors
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Propagators, loop integrals, running coupling, renormalized parameters
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: Path integral in position space: exp(-S) = EML-1; full propagator ∫dk → EML-3 (Green's function)
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Landau pole (λ→∞), confinement (g→∞ at Λ_QCD), instantons exp(-1/g²)

connections.to_session_57: Confinement = EML Phase Transition (same theorem as Ising at T_c)
connections.to_session_61: Session 61 free field QFT: EML-1 amplitudes. Session 75: interactions add EML-2 corrections.
connections.to_session_60: Running coupling A(μ) = EML-2 = same class as log partition function A(θ) in info theory
connections.to_session_64: Feynman-Kac (Session 64): same EML-1 → EML-3 bridge via path integral
